rationale: The citing paper relies on Hoeffler (2003) in its theory section to define and distinguish really new innovations versus incrementally new innovations, which is central to its moderator logic. It also cites Hoeffler to support key premises about RNIs (e.g., greater uncertainty/risk and adoption difficulty), and these premises are used directly in hypothesis development. This is more than a passing background mention because the paper's core predictions and interpretation of results are structured around that prior conceptualization.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Hoeffler (2003) as a conceptual foundation for innovation newness, especially the RNI versus INI distinction and associated adoption barriers. It then develops a new argument that innovation locus (core vs peripheral) changes adoption outcomes within that established newness framework.
